seoTitle: Point Clare vs Point Piper — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Point Clare and Point Piper. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Point Clare vs Point Piper: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Point Clare and Point Piper.
heading: Point Clare vs Point Piper — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Point Clare, New South Wales and Point Piper, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Point Clare has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Point Piper (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Point Clare or Point Piper safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Point Clare has a flood risk score of 25/100, while Point Piper scores 21/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Point Clare scores 34/100 for bushfire risk. Point Piper scores 54/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Point Clare or Point Piper?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Point Clare has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.45% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
